Career History

Work Experience

Jun 2024 — Present
SolutionAir Limited
Electrical Engineering Designer / Technologist
Winnipeg, MB
  • Lead electrical design for custom HVAC production orders: schematics, wiring diagrams, and BOMs in SolidWorks Electrical and AutoCAD for direct use in manufacturing.
  • Integrate PLC and DDC control systems with VFDs (Rockwell PowerFlex 525 & 750) and BAS protocols including BACnet, Modbus, and LON.
  • Apply UL and NEC requirements throughout design; conduct internal reviews before customer submission to catch compliance gaps before they reach the shop floor.
  • Developed the company's first formal electrical SOP, standardizing release procedures, BOM approval workflows, and revision control across the design group.
  • Coordinate with manufacturing, supply chain, and quality teams across the full production cycle; support commissioning and live troubleshooting as needed.
  • Applied SCCR and arc-flash training to evaluate fault current, select protection devices, and verify rating plate documentation.
⚡ Built template library → 20% reduction in engineering lead time
2025 — 2026
Change Innovators
Leadership Program Participant
Remote
  • Selected for a year-long professional development program focused on technical leadership, communication, and cross-functional collaboration in engineering environments.
  • Applied leadership frameworks to real project contexts: change management, stakeholder communication, and team coordination.
Jun 2023 — May 2024
Agri-Food Automation Inc.
Control System Specialist
Lethbridge, AB
  • Programmed PLCs across five platforms (Allen Bradley, Siemens, Mitsubishi, Omron, Delta) and developed HMI and SCADA applications in FactoryTalk View and Ignition for food processing facilities.
  • Configured plant-wide networks using Stratix managed switches, Ethernet/IP, Modbus TCP/IP, and Profibus; designed firewall-protected, segmented architectures.
  • Carried out on-site commissioning at four plants: loop checks, PID tuning, sensor calibration, and live troubleshooting across Western Canada.
  • Led migration of a FactoryTalk platform to Ignition — imported tens of thousands of tags, rebuilt full HMI visualization, delivered in 3 months against a 4-month timeline.
  • Served as senior engineer on-site during large facility commissioning, managing a junior engineer and team of electricians with remote support from the principal engineer.
🏆 Ignition HMI recognized as cleanest platform in southern Alberta
May 2019 — Jan 2020
MGVCL (Madhya Gujarat Vij Company)
Electrical Engineering Intern
Vadodara, Gujarat, India
  • Supported SCADA monitoring (WinCC) in a live power distribution environment; helped configure alarms and real-time data displays for grid operators.
  • Tested and calibrated energy meters and CT/PT units in a high-voltage lab; assisted with underground cable testing and distribution equipment maintenance.
  • Worked alongside senior engineers on RTU, switchgear, and HV/LV transmission systems, building foundational knowledge in grid operations and protective devices.
Selected Work

Key Projects

SolutionAir Limited
Mission-Critical Facility — Electrical & Controls Design

Selected as lead designer for a confidential, high-priority mission-critical facility. Full scope: power distribution, control panel schematics, wiring diagrams, and BOM development. Presented design progress in regular client meetings, managed strict 3% air leakage acceptance criteria through multiple design iterations coordinated with manufacturing.

✓ Achieved leakage target — secured the contract for the company
SolidWorks ElectricalAutoCAD Power DistributionRisk Assessment
SolutionAir Limited
Electrical Design Standardization Program

Audited the full HVAC drawing library and identified inconsistencies in symbol usage, wiring conventions, and BOM structure. Built a standardized template set in SolidWorks Electrical covering schematics, wiring diagrams, and BOMs. Collaborated with manufacturing to confirm templates matched actual assembly requirements before rollout.

✓ 20% reduction in engineering lead time; rework decreased within Q1
SolidWorks ElectricalSOP Development QualityECO Control
SolutionAir Limited
Multi-Zone HVAC Control System Design

Designed the complete electrical and control package for a high-complexity HVAC order: multi-zone DDC control, VFD integration, BACnet communication, and coordination with a customer-provided BMS. Resolved ambiguous control requirements across multiple spec review rounds before finalizing drawings.

✓ Zero electrical rework through manufacturing and commissioning
DDCBACnet VFD IntegrationBMS Coordination
Agri-Food Automation Inc.
Ignition HMI Platform Migration

Led migration of an existing FactoryTalk platform to Ignition for a large food processing facility. Connected Ignition OPC/UA server to PLC, imported tens of thousands of tags, rebuilt full HMI visualization from receiving to delivery, and set up alarm management and historian structure.

✓ Delivered 1 month early — recognized as cleanest HMI in southern Alberta
IgnitionOPC/UA FactoryTalkTag Management
Agri-Food Automation Inc.
Plant-Wide Network & Controls Upgrade

Assessed a large food processing facility's control network and identified reliability failures caused by aging unmanaged switches and an unsegmented architecture. Replaced infrastructure with Stratix managed switches and a firewall-protected design, creating separate zones for PLC, HMI, and plant IT traffic.

✓ Network-related PLC faults dropped to zero in three months post-rollout
Stratix SwitchesEthernet/IP Modbus TCP/IPFirewall
Agri-Food Automation Inc.
Multi-Site PLC & HMI Commissioning

Commissioned Allen Bradley and Siemens PLC systems at four food processing sites across Western Canada. Configured site-specific HMI screens in FactoryTalk View and Ignition, performed loop checks, PID tuning, and instrumentation calibration at each location before handover to operations.

✓ All four sites live on schedule — zero safety incidents across all engagements

Automation Programming Assessment

Technical Work Samples

The following seven answers were completed as part of a timed Controls Programming technical assessment. They cover PLC programming, motion control, analog signal scaling, safety relay wiring, and industrial vision system design.

View ↗
Q1
Ladder to Structured Text

IEC 61131-3 conversion of a 3-rung seal-in/latch ladder diagram into equivalent Structured Text with set-reset pattern.

View ↗
Q2
Motion Profile — Trapezoidal

Velocity-time profile for 1s accel / 7s constant / 2s decel. Total distance 8500 mm. S-curve smoothing analysis included.

View ↗
Q3
3-Axis Pick & Place Sequence

Full state-machine sequence in Structured Text for a pneumatic pick and place — all interlocks and safety constraints included.

View ↗
Q4
4-20 mA Analog Scaling

15-bit ADC scaling for a 0–150 psi transducer on a 0–20 mA input card. Full formula derivation with Z = 6554, M = 32767.

View ↗
Q5
Cat 3 Safety Relay Schematic

Dual-channel E-stop, manual reset, and start/stop motor control circuit using a generic safety relay drawn to IEC standards.

View ↗
Q6
Pneumatic Lift Table Safety

Two mechanical solutions (locking pin and POCV) to prevent a pneumatic lift table dropping on E-stop air-loss.

View ↗
Q7
Machine Vision — Focal Length

When to choose a shorter focal length: working distance, field of view, and depth-of-field tradeoffs for industrial vision systems.
